│   ├── rename.rs                # rename tests

### 3. Symlink Tests

### 4. Cancel-Correctness Tests
- Cancel during read/write

### 5. Platform Tests
- Verify io_uring path on Linux

### 6. Lab Runtime Tests
- Deterministic file operations
- Virtual time for delays
- Schedule exploration

- TRACE: Individual syscalls/SQEs
- DEBUG: Operation start/end
- INFO: Test progress, throughput
- WARN: Slow operations, partial writes
- ERROR: Failures with context

 {"id":"bd-2c2i","title":"Lease manager: time-bounded resource claims","description":"# Lease Manager Implementation

## Goal

Implement a lease system for time-bounded claims on remote resources, preventing orphaned resources when nodes fail.

## Background

In distributed systems, resources can be orphaned when:
1. Client crashes without releasing resource
2. Network partition prevents release message
3. Client hangs indefinitely

Leases solve this: resources are only held for a bounded time. If not renewed, they're automatically released.

## Core Types

### Lease
```rust
pub struct Lease {
    /// Unique lease identifier
    id: LeaseId,
    
    /// Resource being leased
    resource: ResourceId,
    
    /// Holder of the lease
    holder: NodeId,
    
    /// When lease was granted
    granted_at: LogicalTime,
    
    /// When lease expires (must renew before)
    expires_at: LogicalTime,
    
    /// Lease state
    state: LeaseState,
}

pub enum LeaseState {
    Active,
    Expiring,  // Close to expiry, renewal needed
    Expired,   // Resource released
    Released,  // Holder explicitly released
}
```

### LeaseId
```rust
pub struct LeaseId {
    /// Resource ID
    resource: ResourceId,
    
    /// Unique grant nonce
    nonce: u64,
    
    /// Granting node
    grantor: NodeId,
}
```

## LeaseManager

```rust
pub struct LeaseManager {
    /// Active leases held by this node
    held: HashMap<LeaseId, Lease>,
    
    /// Leases granted by this node
    granted: HashMap<LeaseId, Lease>,
    
    /// Background renewal task
    renewal_task: TaskHandle,
    
    /// Clock source
    clock: Box<dyn LogicalClock>,
}

impl LeaseManager {
    /// Request a lease on a resource
    async fn acquire(&mut self, resource: ResourceId, duration: Duration) -> Result<Lease, LeaseError>;
    
    /// Renew an existing lease
    async fn renew(&mut self, lease: &mut Lease, duration: Duration) -> Result<(), LeaseError>;
    
    /// Release a lease early
    async fn release(&mut self, lease: Lease) -> Result<(), LeaseError>;
    
    /// Check if lease is still valid
    fn is_valid(&self, lease: &Lease) -> bool;
    
    /// Get time until expiry
    fn time_until_expiry(&self, lease: &Lease) -> Duration;
}
```

## Renewal Strategy

```rust
pub struct RenewalConfig {
    /// Renew when this fraction of lease time remains
    renewal_threshold: f64,  // e.g., 0.5 = renew at half-time
    
    /// Retry interval if renewal fails
    retry_interval: Duration,
    
    /// Max retries before giving up
    max_retries: u32,
}
```

Background task renews leases automatically:
1. Monitor all held leases
2. When time_until_expiry < threshold, renew
3. On failure, retry with backoff
4. If max_retries exceeded, trigger lease_lost callback

## Lease Lost Handling

When a lease expires without renewal:
```rust
pub trait LeaseObserver {
    /// Called when lease cannot be renewed
    fn on_lease_lost(&self, lease: LeaseId, reason: LeaseError);
}
```

Default behavior: cancel tasks/actors using the leased resource.

## Clock Considerations

Leases use logical time, not wall time:
- Prevents issues from clock skew
- Lease duration is 'messages' not 'seconds'
- Renewal piggybacks on normal communication

Alternative: Hybrid logical clocks for bounded skew tolerance.

## Wire Protocol

### LeaseRequest
```rust
struct LeaseRequest {
    resource: ResourceId,
    duration: Duration,
    holder: NodeId,
}
```

### LeaseResponse
```rust
enum LeaseResponse {
    Granted { lease: Lease },
    Denied { reason: LeaseDenied },
}

enum LeaseDenied {
    ResourceNotFound,
    AlreadyLeased { holder: NodeId, expires: LogicalTime },
    ResourceLimits,
}
```

## Integration Points

- RemoteHandle holds Lease for remote task
- ActorRef for remote actors has Lease
- Distributed regions use leases for membership
- Saga steps may acquire leases on resources

## Testing

- Lease acquisition and release
- Automatic renewal in background
- Lease expiry when holder crashes
- Multiple lease requests (conflict handling)
- Lab runtime with simulated network delays

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Lease and LeaseId types
- [ ] LeaseManager with acquire/renew/release
- [ ] Background renewal task
- [ ] LeaseState transitions
- [ ] LeaseObserver callback
- [ ] Wire protocol messages
- [ ] Logical clock integration
- [ ] Tests for all scenarios","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:22:06.196253260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:42:55.317154352Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:42:55.317069584Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distributed","leases","phase4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2c2i","depends_on_id":"bd-1jis","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2c2i","depends_on_id":"bd-3sst","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2dbf","title":"HPACK: Huffman invalid padding silently accepted (FIXED)","description":"# Bug Fix: Huffman Invalid Padding (COMPLETED)

## Status: FIXED in session 2026-01-31

## Location
`src/http/h2/hpack.rs` - huffman_decode function

## Vulnerability
The Huffman decoder silently accepted invalid padding (not all 1s) at the end
of encoded data. Per RFC 7541, padding must be the most-significant bits of EOS.
Invalid padding could indicate encoding errors or malicious data.

## Original Code
```rust
// End of decoding - didn't check padding validity
Ok(String::from_utf8(output).map_err(|_| H2Error::compression("invalid UTF-8"))?)
```

## Fix Applied
Validate padding bits are all 1s:

```rust
// After decoding, check remaining bits are valid padding
if bit_offset > 0 {
    // Remaining bits should be all 1s (padding is MSBs of EOS symbol)
    let padding_mask = (1u8 << (8 - bit_offset)) - 1;
    let padding_bits = current_byte & padding_mask;
    if padding_bits != padding_mask {
        return Err(H2Error::compression("invalid Huffman padding"));
    }
}
```

## Testing Required
- test_huffman_decode_invalid_padding
- test_huffman_decode_valid_padding
- Fuzz test: Random byte sequences","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:57:13.121666105Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T02:57:31.914317531Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T02:57:31.914150501Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2dbf","depends_on_id":"bd-1bic","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}

## Overview

multiple auth plugins, and cancel-correct query semantics.

## Design

### Connection

### Authentication Plugins
```rust
impl MySqlConnection {
    async fn authenticate(&mut self, user: &str, password: &str, auth_plugin: &str) -> Outcome<(), MySqlError> {
        match auth_plugin {
            "caching_sha2_password" => self.auth_caching_sha2(user, password).await,
            "mysql_native_password" => self.auth_native(user, password).await,
            _ => Err(MySqlError::UnsupportedAuthPlugin(auth_plugin.to_string())),

    async fn auth_caching_sha2(&mut self, user: &str, password: &str) -> Outcome<(), MySqlError> {

```

### Queries
```rust
impl MySqlConnection {
    /// Execute query (text protocol).
    pub async fn query(&mut self, cx: &Cx, sql: &str) -> Outcome<Vec<Row>, MySqlError> {
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        
        // Send COM_QUERY
        self.send(Command::Query(sql)).await?;
        
        // Receive column count, column definitions, rows
        let column_count = self.recv_length_encoded_int().await?;
        let columns = self.recv_column_definitions(column_count).await?;
        
        let mut rows = Vec::new();
        loop {
            match self.recv_row_or_eof().await? {
                Some(data) => rows.push(Row::from_data(&columns, data)),
                None => break,  // EOF
            }
        }
        
        Ok(rows)
    }
    
    /// Execute prepared statement.
    pub async fn execute(
        &mut self,
        cx: &Cx,
        sql: &str,
        params: &[Value],
    ) -> Outcome<u64, MySqlError> {
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        
        // COM_STMT_PREPARE
        let stmt_id = self.prepare(sql).await?;
        
        // COM_STMT_EXECUTE
        self.send(Command::StmtExecute { stmt_id, params }).await?;
        
        // Handle result
        let affected = self.recv_ok_packet().await?.affected_rows;
        
        // COM_STMT_CLOSE
        self.send(Command::StmtClose(stmt_id)).await?;
        
        Ok(affected)
    }
}
```

### Transactions
```rust
impl MySqlConnection {
    /// Begin transaction.
    pub async fn begin(&mut self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<Transaction<'_>, MySqlError> {
        self.query(cx, "START TRANSACTION").await?;
        Ok(Transaction { conn: self, committed: false })
    }
}

 {"id":"bd-2vt","title":"[Epoch] Integrate Epochs with Existing Combinators","description":"# Bead asupersync-2vt: Integrate Epochs with Existing Combinators

## Overview and Purpose

This bead extends the existing combinator infrastructure (`join`, `select`, `race`, `bulkhead`, `circuit_breaker`) to be epoch-aware. Epochs define time-bounded windows in distributed systems where operations and symbols remain valid. Making combinators epoch-aware enables:

1. **Epoch boundaries in combinators**: Operations automatically abort or complete when epoch transitions occur
2. **Epoch-scoped operations**: All child futures in a combinator share the same epoch context
3. **Epoch propagation**: Epoch constraints flow through nested combinator trees
4. **Graceful epoch transitions**: Clean cleanup when entering a new epoch

The existing combinator system (particularly `JoinState`, `RaceState`, `SelectState`) provides the foundation for multi-future coordination. This bead adds epoch awareness without breaking existing semantics.

## Core Types

```rust
//! Epoch-aware combinator extensions.
//!
//! This module extends the existing combinators (join, race, select) to support
//! epoch boundaries and epoch-scoped operations.

use crate::combinator::{JoinState, RaceState, SelectState};
use crate::cx::Cx;
use crate::error::{Error, ErrorKind};
use crate::types::Time;
use std::future::Future;
use std::pin::Pin;
use std::task::{Context, Poll};

/// Identifier for an epoch in the distributed system.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash)]
pub struct EpochId(pub u64);

impl EpochId {
    /// The initial epoch.
    pub const GENESIS: Self = Self(0);

    /// Returns the next epoch.
    #[must_use]
    pub const fn next(self) -> Self {
        Self(self.0 + 1)
    }

    /// Returns true if this epoch is before another.
    #[must_use]
    pub const fn is_before(self, other: Self) -> bool {
        self.0 < other.0
    }
}

impl std::fmt::Display for EpochId {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        write!(f, "Epoch({})", self.0)
    }
}

/// Context for epoch-scoped operations.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct EpochContext {
    /// Current epoch ID.
    pub epoch_id: EpochId,

    /// Epoch start time.
    pub started_at: Time,

    /// Epoch deadline (when this epoch ends).
    pub deadline: Time,

    /// Maximum operations allowed in this epoch.
    pub operation_budget: Option<u32>,

    /// Operations executed in this epoch.
    pub operations_used: u32,
}

impl EpochContext {
    /// Creates a new epoch context.
    pub fn new(epoch_id: EpochId, started_at: Time, deadline: Time) -> Self {
        Self {
            epoch_id,
            started_at,
            deadline,
            operation_budget: None,
            operations_used: 0,
        }
    }

    /// Sets an operation budget for this epoch.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn with_operation_budget(mut self, budget: u32) -> Self {
        self.operation_budget = Some(budget);
        self
    }

    /// Returns true if the epoch has expired at the given time.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn is_expired(&self, now: Time) -> bool {
        now > self.deadline
    }

    /// Returns true if the operation budget is exhausted.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn is_budget_exhausted(&self) -> bool {
        match self.operation_budget {
            Some(budget) => self.operations_used >= budget,
            None => false,
        }
    }

    /// Records an operation and returns true if budget allows.
    pub fn record_operation(&mut self) -> bool {
        if self.is_budget_exhausted() {
            return false;
        }
        self.operations_used += 1;
        true
    }

    /// Returns remaining time in this epoch.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn remaining_time(&self, now: Time) -> Option<Time> {
        if now >= self.deadline {
            None
        } else {
            Some(Time::from_nanos(self.deadline.as_nanos() - now.as_nanos()))
        }
    }
}

/// Behavior when an epoch transition occurs.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum EpochTransitionBehavior {
    /// Abort all pending operations immediately.
    AbortAll,

    /// Allow currently-executing operations to complete, but abort waiting ones.
    DrainExecuting,

    /// Fail with an error.
    Fail,

    /// Ignore epoch transitions (for epoch-agnostic operations).
    Ignore,
}

impl Default for EpochTransitionBehavior {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self::AbortAll
    }
}

/// Policy for epoch-aware combinators.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct EpochPolicy {
    /// Behavior when epoch transitions during operation.
    pub on_transition: EpochTransitionBehavior,

    /// Whether to check epoch on each poll.
    pub check_on_poll: bool,

    /// Whether to propagate epoch context to child futures.
    pub propagate_to_children: bool,

    /// Grace period after epoch deadline before hard abort.
    pub grace_period: Option<Time>,
}

impl Default for EpochPolicy {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            on_transition: EpochTransitionBehavior::AbortAll,
            check_on_poll: true,
            propagate_to_children: true,
            grace_period: None,
        }
    }
}

impl EpochPolicy {
    /// Creates a strict policy that aborts immediately on epoch transition.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn strict() -> Self {
        Self {
            on_transition: EpochTransitionBehavior::AbortAll,
            check_on_poll: true,
            propagate_to_children: true,
            grace_period: None,
        }
    }

    /// Creates a lenient policy that drains executing operations.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn lenient() -> Self {
        Self {
            on_transition: EpochTransitionBehavior::DrainExecuting,
            check_on_poll: false,
            propagate_to_children: true,
            grace_period: Some(Time::from_millis(100)),
        }
    }

    /// Creates an ignore policy for epoch-agnostic operations.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn ignore() -> Self {
        Self {
            on_transition: EpochTransitionBehavior::Ignore,
            check_on_poll: false,
            propagate_to_children: false,
            grace_period: None,
        }
    }
}

/// Wrapper that makes any future epoch-aware.
#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct EpochScoped<F> {
    inner: F,
    epoch_ctx: EpochContext,
    policy: EpochPolicy,
    started: bool,
}

impl<F> EpochScoped<F> {
    /// Wraps a future with epoch awareness.
    pub fn new(inner: F, epoch_ctx: EpochContext, policy: EpochPolicy) -> Self {
        Self {
            inner,
            epoch_ctx,
            policy,
            started: false,
        }
    }

    /// Returns the current epoch context.
    pub fn epoch_context(&self) -> &EpochContext {
        &self.epoch_ctx
    }
}

impl<F: Future> Future for EpochScoped<F> {
    type Output = Result<F::Output, EpochError>;

    fn poll(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<Self::Output> {
        // Safety: We don't move inner
        let this = unsafe { self.get_unchecked_mut() };

        // Check epoch validity on first poll
        if !this.started {
            this.started = true;
            if !this.epoch_ctx.record_operation() {
                return Poll::Ready(Err(EpochError::BudgetExhausted));
            }
        }

        // Check epoch on each poll if policy requires
        if this.policy.check_on_poll {
            // In real implementation, would check current time
            // Here we assume epoch_ctx.is_expired() is checked externally
        }

        // Poll the inner future
        let inner = unsafe { Pin::new_unchecked(&mut this.inner) };
        match inner.poll(cx) {
            Poll::Ready(output) => Poll::Ready(Ok(output)),
            Poll::Pending => Poll::Pending,
        }
    }
}

/// Error types for epoch operations.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum EpochError {
    /// Epoch expired during operation.
    Expired { epoch: EpochId },

    /// Operation budget exhausted.
    BudgetExhausted,

    /// Epoch transition occurred.
    TransitionOccurred {
        from: EpochId,
        to: EpochId,
    },

    /// Operation rejected due to epoch mismatch.
    Mismatch {
        expected: EpochId,
        actual: EpochId,
    },
}

impl std::fmt::Display for EpochError {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        match self {
            Self::Expired { epoch } => write!(f, "epoch {} expired", epoch),
            Self::BudgetExhausted => write!(f, "epoch operation budget exhausted"),
            Self::TransitionOccurred { from, to } => {
                write!(f, "epoch transition from {} to {}", from, to)
            }
            Self::Mismatch { expected, actual } => {
                write!(f, "epoch mismatch: expected {}, got {}", expected, actual)
            }
        }
    }
}

impl std::error::Error for EpochError {}

impl From<EpochError> for Error {
    fn from(e: EpochError) -> Self {
        match e {
            EpochError::Expired { .. } => Error::new(ErrorKind::LeaseExpired)
                .with_context(e.to_string()),
            EpochError::BudgetExhausted => Error::new(ErrorKind::CostQuotaExhausted)
                .with_context(e.to_string()),
            EpochError::TransitionOccurred { .. } => Error::new(ErrorKind::Cancelled)
                .with_context(e.to_string()),
            EpochError::Mismatch { .. } => Error::new(ErrorKind::InvalidStateTransition)
                .with_context(e.to_string()),
        }
    }
}

/// State for epoch-aware join combinator.
#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct EpochJoinState<T, const N: usize> {
    /// Underlying join state.
    inner: JoinState<T, N>,

    /// Epoch context shared by all futures.
    epoch_ctx: EpochContext,

    /// Policy for epoch handling.
    policy: EpochPolicy,

    /// Number of futures completed.
    completed: usize,

    /// Whether any future was aborted due to epoch.
    epoch_aborted: bool,
}

impl<T, const N: usize> EpochJoinState<T, N> {
    /// Creates a new epoch-aware join state.
    pub fn new(epoch_ctx: EpochContext, policy: EpochPolicy) -> Self {
        Self {
            inner: JoinState::new(),
            epoch_ctx,
            policy,
            completed: 0,
            epoch_aborted: false,
        }
    }

    /// Records completion of a future.
    pub fn mark_completed(&mut self, index: usize) {
        self.inner.mark_completed(index);
        self.completed += 1;
    }

    /// Returns true if all futures have completed.
    pub fn all_completed(&self) -> bool {
        self.inner.all_completed()
    }

    /// Returns true if the epoch has expired.
    pub fn check_epoch(&self, now: Time) -> Result<(), EpochError> {
        if self.policy.on_transition == EpochTransitionBehavior::Ignore {
            return Ok(());
        }

        if self.epoch_ctx.is_expired(now) {
            // Check grace period
            if let Some(grace) = self.policy.grace_period {
                let grace_deadline = Time::from_nanos(
                    self.epoch_ctx.deadline.as_nanos() + grace.as_nanos()
                );
                if now <= grace_deadline {
                    return Ok(());
                }
            }
            return Err(EpochError::Expired {
                epoch: self.epoch_ctx.epoch_id,
            });
        }

        Ok(())
    }

    /// Handles epoch transition based on policy.
    pub fn handle_transition(&mut self, new_epoch: EpochId) -> Result<(), EpochError> {
        match self.policy.on_transition {
            EpochTransitionBehavior::AbortAll => {
                self.epoch_aborted = true;
                Err(EpochError::TransitionOccurred {
                    from: self.epoch_ctx.epoch_id,
                    to: new_epoch,
                })
            }
            EpochTransitionBehavior::DrainExecuting => {
                // Allow completion but prevent new work
                self.epoch_ctx.operation_budget = Some(0);
                Ok(())
            }
            EpochTransitionBehavior::Fail => {
                Err(EpochError::TransitionOccurred {
                    from: self.epoch_ctx.epoch_id,
                    to: new_epoch,
                })
            }
            EpochTransitionBehavior::Ignore => Ok(()),
        }
    }
}

            epoch_ctx,
            policy,
            winner: None,

    /// Checks if any future has completed (won the race).
    pub fn check_winner(&self) -> Option<usize> {
        self.winner
    }

    /// Records a winner.
    pub fn set_winner(&mut self, index: usize) {
        if self.winner.is_none() {
            self.winner = Some(index);
        }
    }

    /// Checks epoch validity.
    pub fn check_epoch(&self, now: Time) -> Result<(), EpochError> {
        if self.policy.on_transition == EpochTransitionBehavior::Ignore {

        }

        if self.epoch_ctx.is_expired(now) {
            return Err(EpochError::Expired {
                epoch: self.epoch_ctx.epoch_id,
            });
        }

        Ok(())
    }

/// Synchronization primitive for epoch transitions.
///
/// Allows multiple tasks to coordinate at epoch boundaries.
#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct EpochBarrier {
    /// Current epoch.
    epoch: EpochId,

    /// Number of waiters registered.
    waiters: u32,

    /// Number of waiters that have arrived.
    arrived: u32,

    /// Whether the barrier has been triggered.
    triggered: bool,
}

impl EpochBarrier {
    /// Creates a new epoch barrier.
    pub fn new(epoch: EpochId, waiters: u32) -> Self {
        Self {
            epoch,
            waiters,
            arrived: 0,
            triggered: false,
        }
    }

    /// Registers arrival at the barrier.
    ///
    /// Returns `Ok(true)` if this was the last waiter (barrier triggered),
    /// `Ok(false)` if more waiters are expected.
    pub fn arrive(&mut self) -> Result<bool, EpochError> {
        if self.triggered {
            return Err(EpochError::TransitionOccurred {
                from: self.epoch,
                to: self.epoch.next(),
            });
        }

        self.arrived += 1;

        if self.arrived >= self.waiters {
            self.triggered = true;
            Ok(true)
        } else {
            Ok(false)
        }
    }

    /// Returns the number of waiters still expected.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn remaining(&self) -> u32 {
        self.waiters.saturating_sub(self.arrived)
    }

    /// Returns true if the barrier has been triggered.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn is_triggered(&self) -> bool {
        self.triggered
    }

    /// Returns the epoch this barrier is for.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn epoch(&self) -> EpochId {
        self.epoch
    }
}

| `EpochRaceState` | State for epoch-aware race combinator |
| `EpochSelectState` | State for epoch-aware select combinator |
| `EpochBarrier` | Synchronization primitive for epoch boundaries |
| `EpochError` | Error types for epoch operations |

### Key Functions

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `epoch_join()` | Await all futures within epoch constraints |
| `epoch_race()` | Race futures within epoch constraints |
| `epoch_select()` | Select from futures within epoch constraints |
| `EpochScoped::new()` | Wrap any future with epoch awareness |

## Integration Patterns

### Pattern 1: Epoch-Scoped Distributed Fetch

```rust
async fn fetch_symbols_in_epoch(
    cx: &Cx,
    sources: Vec<SourceNode>,
    epoch_ctx: EpochContext,
) -> Result<Vec<Symbol>, Error> {
    let policy = EpochPolicy::strict();

    // Create futures for each source
    let futures: Vec<_> = sources
        .into_iter()
        .map(|src| fetch_from_source(cx, src))
        .collect();

    // Convert to array (simplified; real impl would use const generics or Vec-based join)
    let results = epoch_join(epoch_ctx, policy, futures).await?;

    Ok(results.into_iter().flatten().collect())
}
```

### Pattern 2: Race with Epoch Timeout

```rust
async fn fetch_with_fallback(
    cx: &Cx,
    primary: SourceNode,
    fallback: SourceNode,
    epoch_ctx: EpochContext,
) -> Result<Vec<u8>, Error> {
    let policy = EpochPolicy::lenient();

    let (winner, result) = epoch_race(
        epoch_ctx,
        policy,
        [
            fetch_from_source(cx, primary),
            async {
                // Delay fallback slightly
                sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;
                fetch_from_source(cx, fallback).await
            },
        ],
    ).await?;

    log::info!("Fetch won by source {}", if winner == 0 { "primary" } else { "fallback" });
    result
}
```

### Pattern 3: Epoch Barrier for Consensus

```rust
async fn coordinate_epoch_transition(
    cx: &Cx,
    nodes: &[NodeId],
    current_epoch: EpochId,
) -> Result<EpochId, Error> {
    let mut barrier = EpochBarrier::new(current_epoch, nodes.len() as u32);

    // Notify all nodes to prepare for transition
    for node in nodes {
        notify_epoch_transition(node, current_epoch.next()).await?;
    }

    // Wait for all confirmations
    for _ in nodes {
        let confirmation = receive_confirmation().await?;
        if barrier.arrive()? {
            // We were the last one - barrier triggered
            break;
        }
    }

    Ok(current_epoch.next())
}
```

### Pattern 4: Nested Epoch Contexts

```rust
async fn hierarchical_operation(
    cx: &Cx,
    outer_epoch: EpochContext,
) -> Result<(), Error> {
    let outer_policy = EpochPolicy::strict();

    // Outer operation with epoch constraint
    let result = EpochScoped::new(
        async {
            // Inner operations inherit epoch context
            let inner_epoch = EpochContext::new(
                outer_epoch.epoch_id,
                outer_epoch.started_at,
                // Inner deadline is tighter
                Time::from_nanos(
                    outer_epoch.started_at.as_nanos() +
                    (outer_epoch.deadline.as_nanos() - outer_epoch.started_at.as_nanos()) / 2
                ),
            );

            let inner_policy = EpochPolicy::lenient();

            // Inner join with stricter deadline
            epoch_join(
                inner_epoch,
                inner_policy,
                [subtask_a(), subtask_b()],
            ).await?;

            Ok(())
        },
        outer_epoch,
        outer_policy,
    ).await;

    result.map_err(|e| e.into())
}
```

## Unit Test Scenarios

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    fn epoch_ctx(deadline_ms: u64) -> EpochContext {
        EpochContext::new(
            EpochId(1),
            Time::from_millis(0),
            Time::from_millis(deadline_ms),
        )
    }

    // Test 1: EpochId ordering
    #[test]
    fn test_epoch_id_ordering() {
        let e1 = EpochId(1);
        let e2 = EpochId(2);

        assert!(e1.is_before(e2));
        assert!(!e2.is_before(e1));
        assert!(!e1.is_before(e1));

        assert_eq!(e1.next(), e2);
    }

    // Test 2: EpochContext expiry detection
    #[test]
    fn test_epoch_context_expiry() {
        let ctx = epoch_ctx(1000);

        assert!(!ctx.is_expired(Time::from_millis(500)));
        assert!(!ctx.is_expired(Time::from_millis(1000)));
        assert!(ctx.is_expired(Time::from_millis(1001)));
    }

    // Test 3: Operation budget enforcement
    #[test]
    fn test_operation_budget() {
        let mut ctx = epoch_ctx(1000).with_operation_budget(3);

        assert!(ctx.record_operation()); // 1
        assert!(ctx.record_operation()); // 2
        assert!(ctx.record_operation()); // 3
        assert!(!ctx.record_operation()); // Budget exhausted

        assert!(ctx.is_budget_exhausted());
    }

    // Test 4: Remaining time calculation
    #[test]
    fn test_remaining_time() {
        let ctx = epoch_ctx(1000);

        let remaining = ctx.remaining_time(Time::from_millis(300));
        assert_eq!(remaining, Some(Time::from_millis(700)));

        let remaining = ctx.remaining_time(Time::from_millis(1000));
        assert_eq!(remaining, None);

        let remaining = ctx.remaining_time(Time::from_millis(1500));
        assert_eq!(remaining, None);
    }

    // Test 5: Epoch policy presets
    #[test]
    fn test_epoch_policy_presets() {
        let strict = EpochPolicy::strict();
        assert_eq!(strict.on_transition, EpochTransitionBehavior::AbortAll);
        assert!(strict.check_on_poll);

        let lenient = EpochPolicy::lenient();
        assert_eq!(lenient.on_transition, EpochTransitionBehavior::DrainExecuting);
        assert!(!lenient.check_on_poll);
        assert!(lenient.grace_period.is_some());

        let ignore = EpochPolicy::ignore();
        assert_eq!(ignore.on_transition, EpochTransitionBehavior::Ignore);
        assert!(!ignore.propagate_to_children);
    }

    // Test 6: EpochJoinState epoch checking
    #[test]
    fn test_epoch_join_state_check() {
        let ctx = epoch_ctx(1000);
        let policy = EpochPolicy::strict();
        let state: EpochJoinState<(), 2> = EpochJoinState::new(ctx, policy);

        // Before deadline - OK
        assert!(state.check_epoch(Time::from_millis(500)).is_ok());

        // After deadline - Error
        let result = state.check_epoch(Time::from_millis(1500));
        assert!(matches!(result, Err(EpochError::Expired { .. })));
    }

    // Test 7: EpochJoinState with grace period
    #[test]
    fn test_epoch_join_state_grace_period() {
        let ctx = epoch_ctx(1000);
        let policy = EpochPolicy {
            grace_period: Some(Time::from_millis(100)),
            ..EpochPolicy::strict()
        };
        let state: EpochJoinState<(), 2> = EpochJoinState::new(ctx, policy);

        // During grace period - OK
        assert!(state.check_epoch(Time::from_millis(1050)).is_ok());

        // After grace period - Error
        assert!(state.check_epoch(Time::from_millis(1150)).is_err());
    }

    // Test 8: EpochJoinState transition handling
    #[test]
    fn test_epoch_transition_handling() {
        let ctx = epoch_ctx(1000);

        // AbortAll policy
        let policy = EpochPolicy::strict();
        let mut state: EpochJoinState<(), 2> = EpochJoinState::new(ctx.clone(), policy);
        let result = state.handle_transition(EpochId(2));
        assert!(matches!(result, Err(EpochError::TransitionOccurred { .. })));
        assert!(state.epoch_aborted);

        // DrainExecuting policy
        let policy = EpochPolicy::lenient();
        let mut state: EpochJoinState<(), 2> = EpochJoinState::new(ctx.clone(), policy);
        let result = state.handle_transition(EpochId(2));
        assert!(result.is_ok());
        assert!(state.epoch_ctx.is_budget_exhausted());

        // Ignore policy
        let policy = EpochPolicy::ignore();
        let mut state: EpochJoinState<(), 2> = EpochJoinState::new(ctx, policy);
        let result = state.handle_transition(EpochId(2));
        assert!(result.is_ok());
    }

    // Test 9: EpochBarrier basic operation
    #[test]
    fn test_epoch_barrier_basic() {
        let mut barrier = EpochBarrier::new(EpochId(1), 3);

        assert_eq!(barrier.remaining(), 3);
        assert!(!barrier.is_triggered());

        assert_eq!(barrier.arrive(), Ok(false)); // 1 of 3
        assert_eq!(barrier.remaining(), 2);

        assert_eq!(barrier.arrive(), Ok(false)); // 2 of 3
        assert_eq!(barrier.remaining(), 1);

        assert_eq!(barrier.arrive(), Ok(true)); // 3 of 3 - triggered!
        assert!(barrier.is_triggered());
        assert_eq!(barrier.remaining(), 0);
    }

    // Test 10: EpochBarrier double-trigger prevention
    #[test]
    fn test_epoch_barrier_double_trigger() {
        let mut barrier = EpochBarrier::new(EpochId(1), 1);

        assert_eq!(barrier.arrive(), Ok(true)); // Triggered

        // Further arrivals should error
        let result = barrier.arrive();
        assert!(matches!(result, Err(EpochError::TransitionOccurred { .. })));
    }

    // Test 11: EpochError display
    #[test]
    fn test_epoch_error_display() {
        let expired = EpochError::Expired { epoch: EpochId(5) };
        assert!(expired.to_string().contains("5"));
        assert!(expired.to_string().contains("expired"));

        let budget = EpochError::BudgetExhausted;
        assert!(budget.to_string().contains("budget"));

        let transition = EpochError::TransitionOccurred {
            from: EpochId(1),
            to: EpochId(2),
        };
        assert!(transition.to_string().contains("transition"));

        let mismatch = EpochError::Mismatch {
            expected: EpochId(1),
            actual: EpochId(2),
        };
        assert!(mismatch.to_string().contains("mismatch"));
    }

    // Test 12: EpochError to Error conversion
    #[test]
    fn test_epoch_error_conversion() {
        let expired: Error = EpochError::Expired { epoch: EpochId(1) }.into();
        assert_eq!(expired.kind(), ErrorKind::LeaseExpired);

        let budget: Error = EpochError::BudgetExhausted.into();
        assert_eq!(budget.kind(), ErrorKind::CostQuotaExhausted);

        let transition: Error = EpochError::TransitionOccurred {
            from: EpochId(1),
            to: EpochId(2),
        }.into();
        assert_eq!(transition.kind(), ErrorKind::Cancelled);

        let mismatch: Error = EpochError::Mismatch {
            expected: EpochId(1),
            actual: EpochId(2),
        }.into();
        assert_eq!(mismatch.kind(), ErrorKind::InvalidStateTransition);
    }

    // Test 13: Ignore policy bypasses epoch checks
    #[test]
    fn test_ignore_policy_bypasses_checks() {
        let ctx = epoch_ctx(1000);
        let policy = EpochPolicy::ignore();
        let state: EpochRaceState<(), 2> = EpochRaceState::new(ctx, policy);

        // Even after deadline, ignore policy returns Ok
        assert!(state.check_epoch(Time::from_millis(5000)).is_ok());
    }

    // Test 14: EpochRaceState winner tracking
    #[test]
    fn test_epoch_race_state_winner() {
        let ctx = epoch_ctx(1000);
        let policy = EpochPolicy::strict();
        let mut state: EpochRaceState<(), 3> = EpochRaceState::new(ctx, policy);

        assert!(state.check_winner().is_none());

        state.set_winner(1);
        assert_eq!(state.check_winner(), Some(1));

        // Setting another winner has no effect
        state.set_winner(2);
        assert_eq!(state.check_winner(), Some(1));
    }

    // Test 15: EpochId display formatting
    #[test]
    fn test_epoch_id_display() {
        let epoch = EpochId(42);
        assert_eq!(format!("{}", epoch), "Epoch(42)");
    }
}

## Logging Strategy

```rust
use crate::observability::{LogEntry, LogLevel};

impl EpochContext {
    fn log_created(&self) -> LogEntry {
        LogEntry::debug("Epoch context created")
            .with_field("epoch_id", &format!("{}", self.epoch_id))
            .with_field("deadline_ms", &format!("{}", self.deadline.as_millis()))
            .with_field("operation_budget", &format!("{:?}", self.operation_budget))
    }

    fn log_expired(&self, now: Time) -> LogEntry {
        LogEntry::warn("Epoch expired")
            .with_field("epoch_id", &format!("{}", self.epoch_id))
            .with_field("deadline_ms", &format!("{}", self.deadline.as_millis()))
            .with_field("current_time_ms", &format!("{}", now.as_millis()))
    }

    fn log_budget_exhausted(&self) -> LogEntry {
        LogEntry::info("Epoch operation budget exhausted")
            .with_field("epoch_id", &format!("{}", self.epoch_id))
            .with_field("operations_used", &format!("{}", self.operations_used))
    }
}

impl EpochBarrier {
    fn log_arrive(&self, arrived_count: u32) -> LogEntry {
        LogEntry::debug("Epoch barrier arrival")
            .with_field("epoch_id", &format!("{}", self.epoch))
            .with_field("arrived", &format!("{}", arrived_count))
            .with_field("total", &format!("{}", self.waiters))
    }

    fn log_triggered(&self) -> LogEntry {
        LogEntry::info("Epoch barrier triggered")
            .with_field("epoch_id", &format!("{}", self.epoch))
            .with_field("waiters", &format!("{}", self.waiters))
    }
}

fn log_epoch_transition(from: EpochId, to: EpochId, behavior: EpochTransitionBehavior) -> LogEntry {
    LogEntry::info("Epoch transition")
        .with_field("from_epoch", &format!("{}", from))
        .with_field("to_epoch", &format!("{}", to))
        .with_field("behavior", &format!("{:?}", behavior))
}
